cards.title: Duplicate Integer

enriched.insight: Use a hashmap to count occurrences and check if any count exceeds one.

cards.title: Sort Custom

enriched.insight: Pass a key function to sorted() to order elements by a derived value without mutating the input list.

cards.title: Sort Descending

enriched.insight: Python's sorted() with reverse=True returns a new list in descending order using Timsort.

cards.title: Sort Lambda

enriched.insight: Use sorted with a key lambda to order elements by a derived value without changing the original list.

cards.title: Sorted Copy
cards.code:
from typing import List


def sort_words(words: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    output = sorted(words)
    return output


def sort_numbers(numbers: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    output = sorted(numbers, reverse=True, key=abs)
    return output


# do not modify below this line
original_words = ["cherry", "apple", "blueberry", "banana", "watermelon", "zucchini", "kiwi", "pear"]

print(original_words)
print(sort_words(original_words))

original_numbers = [1, -5, -3, 2, 4, 11, -19, 9, -2, 5, -6, 7, -4, 2, 6]

print(original_numbers)
print(sort_numbers(original_numbers))

enriched.insight: Use sorted with key and reverse to return a new sorted list while leaving the original unchanged.

cards.title: Unpacking
cards.code:
from typing import List, Tuple


def sum_3_integers(triplet: List[int]) -> int:
    a, b, c = triplet
    return a + b + c


def compute_volume(box_dimensions: Tuple[int, int, int]) -> int:
    x, y, z = box_dimensions
    return x * y * z
  

# do not modify below this line
print(sum_3_integers([1, 2, 3]))
print(sum_3_integers([4, 6, 2]))

print(compute_volume((1, 2, 3)))
print(compute_volume((3, 2, 1)))
print(compute_volume((3, 9, 7)))

enriched.insight: Python unpacking binds sequence elements to named variables in one readable statement.
